element_introduction_order.element: Object pulling and throwing (vegetables, mushroom blocks)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary combat alternative to jumping on enemies
Multi-section level design across all worlds
Optional resource collection and world warping
Temporary area access for coins, mushrooms, warps
Required defeat at end of Worlds 1-6
Final boss challenge to complete game

typical_relief_triggers: Sub-space access with safe coin collection
POW Block deployment for screen clearing
Stopwatch activation for enemy freeze periods
Safe platforming sections without immediate threats
typical_tension_triggers: Slippery snow surfaces affecting movement control
Jar entry into unknown Sub-space areas
Vertical scrolling sections with new spatial demands
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Steady escalation through world themes; tension builds via theme-specific hazards and enemy density rather than time pressure; relief provided through optional Sub-space resource gathering and item-based crowd control

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Jars grant entry to Sub-space areas and warps; potions unlock alternate paths
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Sub-space areas accessed via magic potions contain hidden coins, mushrooms, and world-skipping warps

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of four character archetypes with distinct physics (Toad speed vs. Luigi jump height); basic object pulling mechanics in safe context; room-based navigation learning
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Theme-specific hazards introduced (quicksand in deserts, slippery surfaces in snow); expanded tool set with POW blocks and stopwatches; vertical and right-to-left scrolling variants; multi-enemy encounters
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss sequences at world conclusions; combined hazard and enemy pressure; world warping mechanics create non-linear progression paths; final Wart confrontation
notable_spikes: First boss encounter at end of World 1
Snow world slippery surface control requirements

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of seven worlds organized around environmental theme (desert, snow, etc.) that dictates hazards and enemy types; theme consistency reinforces learning curve and spatial recognition
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Desert worlds with quicksand; Snow worlds with slippery surfaces

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels divided into connected sections accessible via doors and ladders, allowing level length expansion without continuous scrolling; encourages door-checking and exploration
Magic potions create temporary access to reflected unscrollable Sub-space areas containing coins and warps to later worlds; optional but incentivized for resource gathering and sequence breaking
End-of-world boss encounters serve as skill gates and narrative beats; six mandatory boss fights (Worlds 1-6) plus final Wart encounter (World 7)

sources_used: tcrf: Yume Koujou: Doki Doki Panic - The Cutting Room Floor
wikipedia: Super Mario Bros. 2 (Gameplay, Plot, and Development sections)
